skipped.component-design: Deferred to the design-analysis co-design stop, where the component map and flow must be built with the human before plan.md.

agenda: Decide the exact specrew start versus SessionStart hook division of labor.
Decide the B2 full-bootstrap versus lightweight welcome-back trigger.
Keep F-171 dispatcher behavior stable while adding a bootstrap provider.
architecture-core.decision: Human delegated intake decision: architecture-core applies at full depth because Proposal 172 changes the primary bootstrap posture and must preserve F-171 dispatcher boundaries while resolving open design questions before planning.
architecture-core.diagram: workshop/architecture-core.md - bootstrap posture and B2 decision boundary notes

agenda: Define SessionStart event input to bootstrap directive output contract.
Define SessionEnd handover-write integration with Proposal 130.
Empirically verify host-specific menu rendering across Claude, Codex, Copilot, and Cursor.
integration-api.decision: Human delegated intake decision: integration-api applies at full depth because the feature composes hook events, generated directives, handover files, launcher behavior, and multiple host-specific interaction surfaces.
integration-api.diagram: workshop/integration-api.md - hook and agent contract sequence notes

agenda: Define visible orientation plus Resume/New/Pick menu sequence.
Resolve structured-picker versus prose-only fallback per host.
Preserve render-before-menu discipline from F-165.
ui-ux.decision: Human delegated intake decision: ui-ux applies at medium depth because the user-visible bootstrap menu and orientation sequence are central, but the detailed menu semantics are composed from Proposal 077, Proposal 078, and Proposal 143.

agenda: Classify session anchors, handover records, and index metadata.
Define validity and freshness checks before resume candidates are surfaced.
Treat absolute worktree path anchors as non-portable.
data-storage.decision: Human delegated intake decision: data-storage applies at medium depth because bootstrap correctness depends on durable session-state and handover files, but Proposal 130 remains the owner of handover format.
data-storage.diagram: workshop/data-storage.md - state and handover relation notes

agenda: Identify trust boundaries for hook event JSON, filesystem state, absolute paths, and generated directives.
Define failure-safe behavior for stale, untrusted, or defunct-worktree anchors.
Prevent hook interactivity or external state from steering unauthorized lifecycle continuation.
security-compliance.decision: Human delegated intake decision: security-compliance applies at medium depth because stale anchors and absolute paths can misdirect session recovery and the hook must stay non-interactive and failure-safe.

agenda: Define journal, breaker, dedupe, and state-classification evidence for B2 bootstrap paths.
Ensure full bootstrap, light welcome-back, and cleared-anchor behavior are diagnosable.
Define failure evidence for missing or invalid handover/session-state files.
observability-resilience.decision: Human delegated intake decision: observability-resilience applies at medium depth because the feature changes startup behavior and must be reviewable through logs, tests, and hook journal evidence.

agenda: Set compatibility and idempotency as design-driving NFRs.
Preserve unchanged B1 and B3 behavior as regression criteria.
Keep B4 pre-compaction capture and Antigravity binding out of scope.
requirements-nfr.decision: Human delegated intake decision: requirements-nfr applies at medium depth because the feature is mostly behavioral posture and compatibility, with measurable outcomes needed for per-host reliability and unchanged-scope claims.

agenda: Define deployment-loop impact for B2 bootstrap provider and SessionEnd handover registration.
Preserve kill-switch and managed-marker behavior from F-171.
Avoid introducing a new install path.
devops-operations.decision: Human delegated intake decision: devops-operations applies at light depth because the feature changes host hook registration and rollout controls, but it should reuse the shipped F-171 deployment loop.
